text:
AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-07

今日结论：
The dominant pattern today is the sustained high attention on AI agent frameworks and coding agents, with 'ai-engineering-from-scratch' (29,471 stars, +595 daily) and 'career-ops' (49,484 stars, +570 daily) leading the pack. Both projects show strong weekly acceleration (8,371 and 1,536 stars respectively), indicating developer interest in practical, production-ready agent tooling and educational curricula. The 'MCP / Tool Calling' category also shows robust weekly growth (avg 4,544 stars), suggesting a shift toward standardized tool interfaces. Meanwhile, 'Distributed Training & Inference Infrastructure' is nearly flat (ColossalAI +2 stars daily), signaling that attention has moved away from infrastructure toward application-layer agent tools. The cross-source signals are weak today, with only two Hacker News posts tangentially related to AI, reinforcing that the action is concentrated on GitHub.

今日精选项目
1. hpcaitech/ColossalAI
   GitHub: https://github.com/hpcaitech/ColossalAI
   Category: Distributed Training & Inference Infrastructure
   Stars: 41,387 (24h +2, 7d +4)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 3x (high, 前 3 日均值 0.7)
   Score: 40 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Colossal-AI is an open-source deep learning system that makes large AI models cheaper, faster, and more accessible through advanced parallelism and heterogeneous memory management.
   Why worth watching: The repo has a sudden breakout with an acceleration of 6 (high), despite a low 7-day average star delta of -0.6. With 41,385 total stars and 4,505 forks, it's a well-established project that recently saw a spike in attention, possibly due to new benchmarks (B200 GPUs showing 50-70% throughput gains) or the release of Open-Sora and DeepSeek fine-tuning guides.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward democratizing large model training: developers can now achieve near-linear scaling on multi-GPU setups with minimal code changes, reducing the barrier to entry for building foundation models.
   Problem solved: Training and deploying large AI models (e.g., LLMs, video generation models) is prohibitively expensive and complex. Colossal-AI reduces cost and time by providing efficient distributed training and inference with minimal code changes.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, recent README updates highlight new benchmarks on B200 GPUs, DeepSeek 671B fine-tuning, and Open-Sora video generation, which may have driven the breakout.
   Target users: AI researchers, ML engineers, and startups working on large-scale model training or inference, especially those using PyTorch and seeking cost-effective GPU utilization.
   Risk notes: The project is backed by a company (HPC-AI Tech) that also offers paid cloud services, which may lead to feature prioritization for commercial users. Additionally, the rapid pace of change in AI infrastructure could make some parallelism strategies obsolete.
   LLM confidence: high

2. rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
   GitHub: https://github.com/rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
   Category: AI Agent Framework / Educational Curriculum
   Stars: 29,471 (24h +595, 7d +8371)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 0.2x (high, 前 3 日均值 2592)
   Score: 83 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production-grade agents, swarms, and MCP servers.
   Why worth watching: With 29,402 stars, a 24h delta of +916, a 7d delta of +8,302, and a three-day average of 3,693 stars/day, this repo is experiencing sustained explosive growth. The acceleration of 0.2 (medium) indicates steady, compounding interest rather than a single viral spike. Over 150,000 readers and 241,000 page views in the last 30 days confirm broad, ongoing engagement.
   Developer takeaway: The market is hungry for deep, practical AI education that goes beyond surface-level tutorials. Projects that combine rigorous fundamentals with production-ready outputs (MCP servers, agents) are gaining traction. This signals a shift toward 'learn by building' and 'ship as you learn' workflows.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Most AI learning resources are scattered, shallow, or API-focused. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end path where every algorithm is built from scratch (raw math first) and every lesson ships a reusable artifact (prompt, skill, agent, or MCP server), bridging the gap between theory and real-world deployment.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed 2026-06-07), and the curriculum continues to expand (503 lessons, 20 phases). The sustained high star velocity over 7 days suggests word-of-mouth and community adoption rather than a one-time event.
   Target users: Software engineers, ML engineers, data scientists, and students who want to understand AI from the ground up and build production-grade AI systems. Suitable for beginners with coding experience through to experienced practitioners looking to fill gaps.
   Risk notes: The curriculum is massive (~320 hours) and may overwhelm casual learners. Some phases are still in progress (indicated by 'coming soon' placeholders). The project's long-term maintenance and completeness depend on the creator's continued effort.
   LLM confidence: high

3. santifer/career-ops
   GitHub: https://github.com/santifer/career-ops
   Category: AI Workflow Automation
   Stars: 49,484 (24h +570, 7d +1536)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 3.3x (high, 前 3 日均值 172.7)
   Score: 82 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An open-source AI-powered job search command center that uses Claude Code and other AI CLIs to evaluate, tailor, and track applications at scale.
   Why worth watching: With 48,963 stars, a 24-hour delta of +223, and a 7-day delta of +1,116, this repo is experiencing sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.3 indicates steady, organic momentum rather than a sudden spike. The project has a recent release and active commits within the last 7 days, signaling ongoing maintenance and community engagement. It's trending because it directly addresses a universal pain point — job hunting — with a practical, AI-driven solution that resonates with a massive audience.
   Developer takeaway: Career-Ops signals a shift toward AI-as-infrastructure for personal productivity. Developers should watch for similar 'AI command center' patterns emerging in other domains (e.g., personal finance, health, learning). The project's architecture — using AI to orchestrate sub-agents, parse unstructured data, and generate structured outputs — is a template for building reliable, user-facing AI tools.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Job seekers waste months manually sifting through hundreds of listings, customizing resumes, and tracking applications. Career-Ops automates the entire pipeline: it scans job portals, evaluates offers against your profile using structured scoring, generates ATS-optimized PDFs, and maintains a single source of truth — all while keeping the human in the loop for final decisions.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The consistent star velocity over the past week (three-day avg +167, seven-day avg +148) and the recent release suggest steady word-of-mouth and community adoption rather than a specific viral event.
   Target users: Job seekers in tech (especially AI/ML roles), career changers, and developers who want to automate their job search without sacrificing quality. Also relevant for recruiters and HR professionals looking to understand AI-driven candidate matching.
   Risk notes: The system relies on third-party AI APIs (Claude, Gemini) which may change pricing or availability. The 'spray-and-pray' warning is important — users must review AI-generated applications to avoid reputational damage. The project's rapid growth may attract low-quality forks or misuse.
   LLM confidence: high

4. esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
   GitHub: https://github.com/esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 18,908 (24h +355, 7d +4285)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 0.6x (high, 前 3 日均值 629.3)
   Score: 81 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go with prefix-cache stability and MCP plugin support.
   Why worth watching: With 18,908 stars, a 24-hour delta of +355, and a 7-day average of +761 stars/day, this project is experiencing sustained high growth. The acceleration of 0.6 indicates steady, organic interest rather than a sudden spike, making it a reliable signal of developer adoption.
   Developer takeaway: The shift to Go and focus on prefix-cache stability signals a trend toward performance-optimized, cost-aware AI tooling. Developers should watch for similar optimizations in other agents as API costs remain a concern.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Provides a configurable, plugin-driven AI coding assistant that minimizes token costs via DeepSeek's prefix caching, supports multi-model orchestration, and offers sandboxed tool execution.
   What changed: The project underwent a ground-up rewrite in Go (v1.0), moving from TypeScript to a single static binary. This major release, combined with active maintenance (latest commit within 7 days) and a recent release within 30 days, likely drove renewed attention.
   Target users: Developers using DeepSeek models who want a terminal-based AI coding assistant with plugin support, sandboxing, and multi-model orchestration.
   Risk notes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-cache behavior; changes to DeepSeek's pricing or API could impact value. The project is still relatively young (v1.0 rewrite) and has 212 open issues, indicating active development but potential instability.
   LLM confidence: high

5. nexu-io/open-design
   GitHub: https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 60,343 (24h +830, 7d +4396)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1.3x (high, 前 3 日均值 640)
   Score: 77 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Open Design is a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns your coding agent into a design engine, generating prototypes, slides, images, and videos from a single design system.
   Why worth watching: With 60,343 stars, a 24-hour delta of +830, and a 7-day average of +662 stars/day, Open Design is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.3 indicates strong but not explosive momentum. The project just shipped v0.9.0 with a built-in Model Router, lowering the barrier to entry. This is a rare case of a design tool that integrates natively with the agent ecosystem — a space that is currently exploding in interest.
   Developer takeaway: Open Design signals a shift toward agent-native design workflows where the design system is code (DESIGN.md) and the agent is the renderer. For AI developers, this means the boundary between 'design tool' and 'development environment' is dissolving. The project's MCP server integration makes it a pluggable component in any agent stack, which could become a standard pattern for tool-augmented agents.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Design tools today are either closed (Figma, Claude Design) or require manual pixel-pushing. Open Design lets developers and designers define a brand contract once in a DESIGN.md file, then have any coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) produce consistent, exportable artifacts — HTML, PDF, PPTX, MP4 — without leaving the terminal or switching apps.
   What changed: The release of v0.9.0 introduced the official Model Router, eliminating the need for API key setup. Combined with the Open Design Fellow program announcement and a growing plugin ecosystem (261 plugins), the project has likely seen a surge in adoption from developers who want to try agent-driven design without friction.
   Target users: Developers and designers who use AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) and want to generate consistent, branded UI artifacts without switching to a separate design tool. Also teams looking for a local-first, open-source alternative to Figma or Claude Design.
   Risk notes: The project is still in early release (v0.9.0) with 329 open issues, which may indicate rough edges. It relies on external coding agents and their APIs, so any changes in those ecosystems could affect functionality. The rapid star growth may attract maintenance pressure. Also, the 'design tool' space is crowded, and adoption beyond the developer community is unproven.
   LLM confidence: high

6. CopilotKit/CopilotKit
   GitHub: https://github.com/CopilotKit/CopilotKit
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 33,310 (24h +540, 7d +1456)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 1.9x (high, 前 3 日均值 287.7)
   Score: 75 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: CopilotKit is a multi-platform SDK for building agent-native applications with Generative UI, shared state, and human-in-the-loop workflows across React, Angular, Vue, React Native, Slack, and Teams.
   Why worth watching: The repo gained 973 stars in the last 24 hours and 1,367 in the last 7 days, with a 7.9x acceleration ratio (high). This sudden breakout from a sustained base of ~33k stars signals a major attention spike, likely driven by a new release or announcement.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward 'agent-native' applications where the UI is dynamically generated by agents, not hardcoded. Developers should consider adopting protocols like AG-UI to future-proof their agent integrations across surfaces.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Developers need a unified frontend stack to integrate AI agents into any application surface, enabling dynamic UI rendering, real-time state synchronization, and human-in-the-loop interactions without building custom infrastructure.
   What changed: The README mentions recent support for Angular, Vue, React Native, Slack, and Microsoft Teams, plus a new 'Self-Learning Agents' feature in early access. The project also introduced the AG-UI Protocol, which has been adopted by Google, LangChain, AWS, Microsoft, Mastra, and PydanticAI. These expansions and partnerships likely triggered the surge.
   Target users: Full-stack developers building AI-powered applications, especially those using React, Angular, Vue, or React Native, and teams deploying agents to Slack or Microsoft Teams.
   Risk notes: The project is still evolving with early access features (Self-Learning, Slack/Teams). The high number of open issues (532) may indicate growing pains. Dependency on the AG-UI protocol could lock users into CopilotKit's ecosystem.
   LLM confidence: high

7. affaan-m/ECC
   GitHub: https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC
   Category: MCP / Tool Calling
   Stars: 209,269 (24h +804, 7d +9948)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 0.5x (high, 前 3 日均值 1524.3)
   Score: 75 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A harness-native operator system for AI agent workflows, providing skills, memory, security, and cross-harness compatibility for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and more.
   Why worth watching: With 209K stars and a 7-day average of 1,633 new stars per day, this project is experiencing sustained, massive attention. The 24-hour delta of 804 stars and acceleration of 0.5 indicate continued high interest without a sudden spike. The recent v2.0.0-rc.1 release adds operator workflows, a dashboard GUI, and Rust control-plane alpha, keeping momentum strong.
   Developer takeaway: The rise of ECC signals a maturing ecosystem where agent workflows are becoming standardized and tooled. Developers should expect more 'agent operating systems' that abstract away harness-specific quirks, enabling portable, production-grade agentic development. The focus on security (AgentShield) and memory persistence addresses key adoption barriers.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Developers using multiple AI coding agents face fragmented configurations, inconsistent behavior, and lack of shared memory or security. ECC provides a unified system of skills, hooks, rules, and MCP configs that work across harnesses, optimizing token usage, memory persistence, and agent orchestration.
   What changed: The v2.0.0-rc.1 release (Apr 2026) introduced a dashboard GUI, operator workflows, prediction-market skills, optimization packs, and the ECC 2.0 Rust alpha. This major feature release likely drove the sustained star growth.
   Target users: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Gemini, Zed, GitHub Copilot) who want to improve agent performance, security, and cross-harness consistency. Also relevant for builders of MCP servers and agentic workflows.
   Risk notes: The project is primarily maintained by a single person (affaan-m), which could pose bus-factor risk despite community contributions. The rapid growth may lead to scalability challenges in issue triage and feature requests. Some features (like ECC Pro) are paid, which may limit adoption for some users.
   LLM confidence: high

8. HKUDS/nanobot
   GitHub: https://github.com/HKUDS/nanobot
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 43,801 (24h +58, 7d +401)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 0.9x (high, 前 3 日均值 68)
   Score: 74 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An ultra-lightweight, open-source personal AI agent that you can truly own, with WebUI, chat channels, tools, memory, MCP, and model routing.
   Why worth watching: With 43,785 stars, a 24h delta of +91, and a 7d delta of +403, the project shows strong, sustained growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.4 indicates steady momentum without a sudden spike. This is a mature, actively maintained project that has been consistently gaining attention for months.
   Developer takeaway: The trend toward lightweight, open-source agent frameworks is accelerating. Developers are moving away from monolithic, closed solutions toward modular, self-hosted agents. nanobot's rapid release cycle and broad provider support signal that the ecosystem is maturing.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Most AI agents are either too heavy, closed-source, or tied to a single provider. nanobot gives developers a small, readable core that can be extended with plugins, supports multiple LLM providers, and runs locally or on your own infrastructure.
   What changed: The most recent release v0.2.1 (The Workbench Release) on 2026-06-01 introduced a packaged WebUI with clearer thought/response timelines, live file-edit activity, project workspaces, and broader provider/channel support. The README shows daily updates for the past month, indicating rapid iteration.
   Target users: Python developers building personal or team AI assistants, AI enthusiasts who want to run their own agent, and developers integrating LLMs into chat workflows.
   Risk notes: The project is very new (first release in April 2026) and still has 863 open issues. The rapid pace of changes may lead to instability. The README is promotional and may oversell capabilities. The project is from HKUDS, which may have limited long-term support.
   LLM confidence: high

9. addyosmani/agent-skills
   GitHub: https://github.com/addyosmani/agent-skills
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 48,767 (24h +289, 7d +1567)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1.3x (high, 前 3 日均值 225.3)
   Score: 74 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A curated pack of 23 production-grade engineering skills packaged as Markdown workflows for AI coding agents.
   Why worth watching: With 48,767 stars and a sustained 7-day average of 259 new stars per day, this project has massive community momentum. The 24-hour delta of 289 stars and acceleration of 1.3 indicate continued strong interest, not a flash-in-the-pan spike. It's becoming a de facto standard for agent skill definitions.
   Developer takeaway: The rise of structured skill packs signals a shift from 'prompt engineering' to 'workflow engineering' for AI agents. Developers who adopt these patterns will get more consistent, higher-quality outputs from their coding agents.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: AI coding agents often produce low-quality code because they lack the structured workflows, quality gates, and best practices that senior engineers follow. This repo encodes those practices into reusable skills that agents can execute consistently.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The high star velocity and recent release within 30 days suggest ongoing community adoption and word-of-mouth spread.
   Target users: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, GitHub Copilot) who want production-quality code generation without reinventing the wheel.
   Risk notes: Skills are tied to specific agent platforms and may not work identically across all of them. The project is opinionated — teams with different workflows may need to adapt. Long-term maintenance by a single author is a bus-factor risk.
   LLM confidence: high

10. sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills
   GitHub: https://github.com/sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills
   Category: MCP / Tool Calling
   Stars: 39,926 (24h +82, 7d +713)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 0.7x (high, 前 3 日均值 115.3)
   Score: 74 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An installable library of 1,520+ reusable SKILL.md playbooks for AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini CLI.
   Why worth watching: With 39,907 stars, a 7-day average delta of 100.1 stars/day, and a 24h delta of 159 stars, the project shows strong sustained growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.5 indicates high but not explosive momentum, suggesting steady organic adoption rather than a viral spike.
   Developer takeaway: The project signals a shift toward standardized, shareable agentic workflows. Developers should consider building or adopting skill libraries to maximize AI tool effectiveness and reduce prompt engineering overhead.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Eliminates the need to craft one-off prompts by providing a searchable, installable catalog of structured agentic skills for common development, testing, security, and workflow tasks.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The latest release (V12.1.0) and consistent commit activity within the last 7 days indicate active maintenance, but no external buzz or sudden acceleration event is detected.
   Target users: Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, Kiro, GitHub Copilot, or OpenCode who want pre-built, structured instructions for common coding, testing, security, and workflow tasks.
   Risk notes: The README is the primary source of information; actual skill quality and compatibility may vary. The project's reliance on a single maintainer and community contributions could affect long-term consistency.
   LLM confidence: high

2. 对比一下 GPT-5.5 的设计效果和 Opus 4.8 的设计效果

   URL: https://x.com/dotey/status/2063464057647075379

   Category: tools
   Summary: 宝玉对比了GPT-5.5与Opus 4.8的设计能力，认为Opus 4.8效果远优于GPT-5.5。他使用了基于Cursor浏览器和元素标注的baoyu-design Skill，该Skill通过npx skills add JimLiu/baoyu-design安装，可在本地运行：描述屏幕需求即可生成精良HTML，点击预览中任意元素即可发出修改指令。官方推荐搭配Opus 4.8以获得最佳效果。工具GitHub仓库：https：//github.com/JimLiu/baoyu-design。

3. M3与Opus代码审计13个bug：$0.07 vs $1.30
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://x.com/MiniMax_AI/status/2063397618034844135
   Signal captured
   Category: tools
   Summary: 对 Claude Opus 4.8 和 MiniMax M3 进行相同的代码审计：同一代码库、同一提示词，预先植入 17 个已知 bug。MiniMax M3 以 $0.07 抓到 13 个；最便宜的 Claude 运行同样抓到 13 个，花费 $1.30。MiniMax 表示这一对比非常有趣，绝对值得一读。
   Original: X：MiniMax (@MiniMax_AI) - https://x.com/MiniMax_AI/status/2063397618034844135

5. 五个实验室，五个心智：用小模型构建多模型金融剧情游戏
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/thousand-token-wood-sim-v2
   Signal captured
   Category: tools
   Summary: Thousand Token Wood v2使用四个不同实验室的小模型（gpt-oss-20b、MiniCPM3-4B、Nemotron-Mini-4B及微调Qwen 0.5B）驱动金融模拟游戏的智能体。核心发现是异构服务层摩擦在于vLLM 0.22.1需CUDA工具包，而非模型本身。通过容忍性JSON解析层，添加模型只需一条配置。信息隔离确保内幕标志不在提示词中，扫描测试验证无泄露。记忆用情绪摘要截断避免淹没。微调0.5B模型实现0%自成交、100%有效报价，真相防火墙零泄露。小模型是可靠格式生成器但不可靠推理器，可通过结构化、提示词和微调弥补。

8. GitHub 开源 Spec Kit 工具包，用产品规范引导 AI 编码
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://x.com/rohanpaul_ai/status/2063246343842501091
   Signal captured
   Category: products
   Summary: GitHub 发布开源工具包 Spec Kit，旨在解决 "vibe coding" 的最大弱点--AI 常在规则未明确时就开始编码。它把流程从 "让 AI 直接构建" 改为 "先写产品规范，再让 AI 根据规范实现"。当前 AI 编码模式常因松散提示直接跳入代码，导致需求薄弱、边界遗漏和反复返工。Spec Kit 推动反向流程：先定义产品功能，再澄清差距、制订技术计划、分解任务，最后让 agent 执行。规范成为可执行的开发合约，支持 Copilot、Claude Code、Codex、Gemini、Cursor、Qwen 等 30+ agent 集成。项目已获 109K+ 星标。
   Original: X：Rohan Paul (@rohanpaul_ai) - https://x.com/rohanpaul_ai/status/2063246343842501091
   Why it matters: GitHub 发布开源工具包 Spec Kit，旨在解决 "vibe coding" 的最大弱点--AI 常在规则未明确时就开始编码。它把流程从 "让 AI 直接构建" 改为 "先写产品规范，再让 AI 根据规范实现"。当前 AI 编码模式常因松散提示直接跳入代码，导致需求薄弱、边界遗漏和反复返工。Spec Kit 推动反向流程：先定义产品功能，再澄清差距、制订技术计划、分解任务，最后让 agent 执行。规范成为可执行的开发合约，支持 Copilot、Claude Code、Codex、Gemini、Cursor、Qwen 等 30+ agent 集成。项目已获 109K+ 星标。

10. Persona Atlas：Hugging Face 上的开源人物思维映射工具
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/persona-atlas
   Signal captured
   Category: products
   Summary: Persona Atlas 是一个运行在 Hugging Face Inference Providers 上的开源项目。它通过工具调用代理执行真实网络搜索，生成公众人物的资料、事实清单和风格假设，然后让该人物回答十个关于身份、伦理等开放式问题。每个回答被转化为嵌入向量，从而在向量空间中对不同人物进行距离比较，并基于十个特质锚点绘制热力图。前端采用 Gradio，提供研究、比较和检查代理完整追溯三个标签页，预设多个人物角色，无需 token 即可直接体验。

11. PixelDiT入选CVPR2026最佳论文决赛
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://x.com/NVIDIAAI/status/2063034422698389625
   Signal captured
   Category: papers
   Summary: 被选为 #CVPR2026 最佳论文决赛作品：来自 NVIDIA Research 的 PixelDiT 在大多数图像生成模型中，预训练的自编码器会在任何扩散发生前压缩图像，导致质量损失在整个流程中累积。 PixelDiT，即像素扩散变换器，完全去掉了这一步骤。它是一个单阶段模型，直接在像素空间中端到端地学习扩散过程。
   Original: X：NVIDIA AI (@NVIDIAAI) - https://x.com/NVIDIAAI/status/2063034422698389625

12. 用Qwen2.5-3B构建多智能体经济体：工程报告
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/thousand-token-wood-sim
   Signal captured
   Category: tools
   Summary: 开发者用Qwen2.5-3B构建了五人森林生物多智能体经济体，每个智能体独立运行，通过vLLM部署在Modal，以Gradio为交互窗口。3B模型在100%调用中输出有效JSON，但经济判断能力弱。通过设计稀缺性（食物品种限制、易腐坏、冬季燃料危机）和优化提示词（禁止买入自产物品、给出示例）提升决策质量。15轮模拟中，蜜价从10跌至3、柴价从4涨至7、财富基尼系数从0.14扩至0.38。项目展示了小模型可靠格式化与不可靠推理之间的工程填补。
   Original: Hugging Face：Blog（RSS） - https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/thousand-token-wood-sim
   Why it matters: 开发者用Qwen2.5-3B构建了五人森林生物多智能体经济体，每个智能体独立运行，通过vLLM部署在Modal，以Gradio为交互窗口。3B模型在100%调用中输出有效JSON，但经济判断能力弱。通过设计稀缺性（食物品种限制、易腐坏、冬季燃料危机）和优化提示词（禁止买入自产物品、给出示例）提升决策质量。15轮模拟中，蜜价从10跌至3、柴价从4涨至7、财富基尼系数从0.14扩至0.38。项目展示了小模型可靠格式化与不可靠推理之间的工程填补。
